SEARCH

Class PokemonCharacter

java.lang.Object
PokemonCharacter
public class PokemonCharacter extends Object
Represents a Pokemon character with various attributes. Supports flexible sorting based on different attributes using Comparators.
Version:
3.0
Author:
Christopher Perez

    • setSortCriteria

      public static void setSortCriteria(PokemonCharacter.SortBy criteria)
      Sets the current sorting criteria for all PokemonCharacter comparisons
      Parameters:
      criteria - The attribute to sort by

    • compareTo

      public int compareTo(PokemonCharacter other)
      Compares this Pokemon to another based on the current sort criteria. Uses a clever approach to handle multiple sorting attributes dynamically.
      Parameters:
      other - The other Pokemon to compare to
      Returns:
      negative if this invalid input: '<' other, 0 if equal, positive if this > other

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • toDisplayString

      public String toDisplayString()
      Returns a formatted string with specific attributes for display
      Returns:
      formatted string with English name, Japanese name, speed, and hit points

      public boolean equals(Object obj)

    • hashCode

      public int hashCode()
      Returns the prime number multiplied by the current reselt by 31
      Overrides:
      hashCode in class Object
      Returns:
      the results of the initial pokemonCharacter value times the prime number 31 and 17 to bitwise shift
